Security Affairs newsletter Round 574 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
Hot take: AI's not going to kill open source code security
Trigona ransomware adopts custom tool to steal data and evade detection
SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 94
GopherWhisper: new China-linked APT targets Mongolia with Go-based malware

executive_brief: The threat landscape has crossed a decisive threshold: artificial intelligence and developer toolchains are no longer secondary attack surfaces, they are the primary vectors for enterprise compromise. This week, attackers weaponized CI/CD pipelines, AI integrations, and legacy infrastructure to bypass traditional perimeter defenses entirely. The Vercel breach and Bitwarden CLI supply chain compromise demonstrate how threat actors hijack trusted automation to harvest cloud credentials and propagate self-replicating malware across developer environments. Simultaneously, the FIRESTARTER backdoor on federal Cisco firewalls proves that standard patching cycles fail against resilient, firmware-embedded persistence mechanisms. Security operations must abandon reactive vulnerability management and implement architectural hardening across AI agents, software supply chains, and edge infrastructure immediately.

threat_stories.headline: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Hijacking: From Vercel to Bitwarden CLI
threat_stories.narrative: Attackers executed a coordinated wave of supply chain compromises targeting developer platforms and package registries, exploiting the blind trust organizations place in automation and AI integrations. On April 19, Vercel confirmed a breach originating from a third-party AI integration hack, granting attackers access to internal environments and customer credentials. The intrusion chain relied on OAuth abuse and compromised employee accounts, bypassing multi-factor authentication by leveraging trusted application tokens. Within days, the same threat ecosystem pivoted to the npm registry. On April 23, researchers identified a trojanized version of the Bitwarden CLI (v2026.4.0) published through a compromised GitHub Action in the CI/CD pipeline. The malicious package installs a custom loader that silently downloads the Bun runtime and executes a 10MB obfuscated credential harvester. The payload scans for SSH keys, cloud provider tokens, and GitHub PATs, then weaponizes stolen tokens to inject malicious workflows and exfiltrate data to fake Checkmarx domains. This campaign eliminates the need for user interaction, automating initial access, credential theft, and lateral movement across development infrastructure.

timeline.event: Vercel confirms breach triggered by third-party AI integration compromise and OAuth abuse.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates how AI platform integrations serve as high-value entry points for internal environment access.

timeline.event: Attackers compromise Bitwarden CI/CD pipeline via malicious GitHub Action.
timeline.significance: Establishes the supply chain vector for distributing trojanized developer tools.

timeline.event: Trojanized @bitwarden/cli v2026.4.0 published to npm registry containing bw_setup.js and bw1.js payloads.
timeline.significance: Initiates automated credential harvesting and self-propagating worm distribution across developer machines.

timeline.event: Security vendors identify Shai-Hulud malware variants and malicious KICS Docker images in the same campaign.
timeline.significance: Confirms broad supply chain targeting spanning npm, Docker, and VS Code extension registries.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Development teams, CI/CD pipelines, and cloud infrastructure face severe compromise. Stolen credentials enable unauthorized repository access, malicious workflow injection, and cloud resource hijacking. Organizations using Bitwarden CLI v2026.4.0 or unverified npm packages experience immediate credential exfiltration and environment takeover.
threat_stories.action_required: Uninstall @bitwarden/cli v2026.4.0 immediately and clear npm caches. Rotate all GitHub PATs, npm tokens, AWS/GCP/Azure keys, and SSH certificates. Audit GitHub Actions workflows for unauthorized injections. Enforce npm --ignore-scripts for untrusted installs and implement strict CI/CD pipeline signing. Isolate compromised CI runners and revoke ambient cloud credentials.

threat_stories.headline: FIRESTARTER Backdoor and Covert IoT Infrastructure Campaign
threat_stories.narrative: Threat actors deployed the FIRESTARTER backdoor against a U.S. federal civilian agency's Cisco Firepower device, demonstrating persistence that survives standard security patches and firmware updates. CISA and the UK NCSC confirmed the malware embeds itself in system-level hooks or configuration backups, maintaining remote access even after administrators apply vendor fixes. This technical capability aligns with a broader infrastructure campaign documented by CISA and the NCSC on April 21. China-linked threat actors recruit unpatched consumer IoT, SOHO routers, and IP cameras into dynamic covert proxy networks. These compromised devices function as multi-hop entry, traversal, and exit nodes, masking true attack origins and bypassing static IP blocklists. The constantly refreshed infrastructure renders traditional IOC-based defenses obsolete. Attackers route reconnaissance, exploitation, and data exfiltration through these covert networks, ensuring rapid IOC extinction and sustained operational resilience across the full cyber kill chain.

timeline.event: CISA reports FIRESTARTER backdoor infection on Cisco ASA/FTD devices in federal networks.
timeline.significance: Confirms patch-resilient persistence mechanisms targeting critical network security appliances.

timeline.event: CISA and NCSC issue joint advisory on China-nexus covert networks utilizing compromised consumer IoT.
timeline.significance: Validates the shift toward dynamic, multi-hop proxy infrastructure that evades static blocking.

timeline.event: Researchers confirm FIRESTARTER survives routine Cisco firewall updates and security patches.
timeline.significance: Proves that standard remediation workflows fail against firmware-embedded backdoors, requiring full reimaging.

timeline.event: Federal agencies and critical infrastructure operators place networks on high alert for covert proxy activity.
timeline.significance: Triggers mandatory infrastructure audits and egress filtering enforcement across government and CNI sectors.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Federal agencies, critical infrastructure, and enterprises relying on perimeter firewalls face compromised network security controls. Covert IoT networks enable undetected lateral movement, credential harvesting, and data exfiltration. Standard patching procedures provide false security against FIRESTARTER persistence.
threat_stories.action_required: Isolate and reimage all Cisco ASA/FTD devices exhibiting FIRESTARTER indicators; patching alone fails. Implement strict egress filtering to block unauthorized C2 communications. Deploy dynamic threat intelligence feeds to filter covert infrastructure indicators. Segment IoT and SOHO devices into isolated VLANs with machine certificate verification. Replace end-of-life firmware devices immediately.

threat_stories.headline: The AI Agent Attack Surface: Prompt Injection and Architectural Flaws
threat_stories.narrative: Organizations deploying AI agents without rigorous security boundaries face immediate data exfiltration and remote code execution. Attackers weaponize indirect prompt injection by embedding malicious instructions in HTML comments, CSS, and metadata across public websites. When AI assistants like GitHub Copilot or enterprise agents process these pages, they execute the hidden directives, leaking sensitive data or manipulating code generation. On April 21, researchers exposed a critical misconfiguration in the Azure SRE Agent's WebSocket endpoint. The hub accepted multi-tenant Entra ID tokens without verifying tenant membership or resource-level authorization, broadcasting all internal reasoning traces, executed commands, and deployment credentials to any connecting client. Simultaneously, the Lovable vibe-coding platform suffered a broken access control flaw allowing free-tier users to enumerate and read other users' source code, credentials, and chat histories. These incidents prove that AI platforms prioritize functionality over isolation, creating bidirectional attack surfaces where compromised agents silently harvest enterprise data.

timeline.event: Lovable platform suffers broken access control enabling cross-account data and source code theft.
timeline.significance: Highlights how rushed AI platform deployments bypass fundamental authorization checks.

timeline.event: Azure SRE Agent misconfiguration exposes multi-tenant WebSocket hub to unauthorized eavesdropping.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates how agentic services leak internal commands, credentials, and reasoning traces.

timeline.event: Google Threat Intelligence documents 32% increase in indirect prompt injection attempts across web content.
timeline.significance: Confirms attackers systematically poison public web assets to manipulate autonomous AI agents.

timeline.event: Researchers identify critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol enabling RCE.
timeline.significance: Proves AI supply chain protocols lack fundamental input validation, risking host system compromise.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise cloud operations, developer environments, and customer data face silent exfiltration. AI agents processing untrusted web content or connecting to misconfigured endpoints leak credentials, internal reasoning, and proprietary code. Organizations lack visibility into agent-driven data flows.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy AI security gateways to sanitize inputs and block hidden prompt instructions. Enforce strict tenant isolation and resource-level authorization for all agentic services. Sandbox AI agent execution environments and restrict outbound network access. Audit API endpoints for broken object-level authorization. Disable AI assistants when browsing untrusted web content.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Vulnerabilities: Pack2TheRoot and Breeze Cache Exploitation
threat_stories.narrative: Threat actors weaponize both decade-old infrastructure flaws and newly disclosed web vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed. The Pack2TheRoot vulnerability (CVE-2026-41651) resurfaces across Linux distributions, allowing local unprivileged users to execute PackageKit daemon commands without authentication. Attackers run pkcon install to drop malicious packages, achieving full root privilege escalation on Ubuntu, Debian, and Fedora systems. The flaw persisted for twelve years due to misconfigured D-Bus policies. Simultaneously, the Breeze Cache WordPress plugin suffers an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload vulnerability (CVE-2026-3844) in its fetch_gravatar_from_remote function. Over 400,000 WordPress sites face immediate compromise as attackers upload web shells and deploy backdoors. Microsoft's ASP.NET Core framework also requires urgent remediation after a regression in the Data Protection Library miscalculates HMAC validation tags, allowing attackers to forge authentication cookies and decrypt protected payloads. Patching windows collapse as automated scanners deploy exploits within hours of disclosure.

timeline.event: Microsoft releases out-of-band patch for ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 HMAC validation flaw.
timeline.significance: Forces immediate rotation of authentication cookies and data protection tokens across .NET environments.

timeline.event: Hackers actively exploit Breeze Cache WordPress plugin CVE-2026-3844 for unauthenticated RCE.
timeline.significance: Triggers mass web shell deployment across hundreds of thousands of internet-facing sites.

timeline.event: Security researchers disclose Pack2TheRoot (CVE-2026-41651) enabling trivial Linux root escalation.
timeline.significance: Exposes systemic D-Bus policy misconfigurations across major Linux distributions.

timeline.event: CISA adds SimpleHelp and Samsung MagicINFO flaws to KEV catalog with federal patch deadlines.
timeline.significance: Mandates immediate remediation for remote support and digital signage infrastructure.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Linux servers, WordPress deployments, and .NET applications face complete system takeover. Unpatched instances experience automated credential theft, backdoor installation, and lateral movement. Organizations delay patching at the cost of immediate compromise.
threat_stories.action_required: Update PackageKit to v1.3.5+ and restrict D-Bus access policies. Patch Breeze Cache to v2.4.5+ and disable unused plugin features. Apply ASP.NET Core v10.0.7+ and rotate all Data Protection tokens. Expire affected authentication cookies immediately. Deploy WAF rules to block unauthorized file uploads and restrict outbound connections from web servers.

actor_profiles.name: UNC6692
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed multistage intrusions using Microsoft Teams helpdesk impersonation. Deployed SNOWBELT browser extension, SNOWGLAZE WebSocket tunnel, and SNOWBASIN Python backdoor. Dumped LSASS memory and exfiltrated NTDS.dit via cloud storage.

actor_profiles.name: GopherWhisper
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Targeted Mongolian government systems with Go-based backdoors. Abused legitimate cloud services and Microsoft Graph API for command and control. Deployed modular Linux malware to maintain persistent access.
targets: South Asian and Central Asian entities

actor_profiles.name: TeamPCP
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial theft and supply chain disruption
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Compromised Bitwarden CLI CI/CD pipeline via malicious GitHub Actions. Distributed trojanized npm package v2026.4.0 containing self-propagating credential harvester. Injected malicious workflows into developer repositories.

actor_profiles.name: NGate Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Trojanized legitimate HandyPay Android application with AI-generated malicious code. Abused NFC permissions to relay and exfiltrate payment data and PINs. Targeted Brazilian users through unofficial distribution channels.

actor_profiles.name: The Gentlemen
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Operated SystemBC C2 infrastructure revealing over 1,570 compromised victims. Deployed custom exfiltration tools to harvest sensitive data before encryption. Maintained persistent access across healthcare and manufacturing sectors.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Missing authentication policies in PackageKit daemon allow local unprivileged users to execute pkcon install/remove commands without passwords, enabling arbitrary package installation and full root privilege escalation.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update PackageKit to version 1.3.5 or later. Restrict D-Bus access policies for PackageKit service to require authentication.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Missing file type validation in fetch_gravatar_from_remote function permits unauthenticated arbitrary file uploads, leading to remote code execution and complete WordPress site takeover.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Breeze Cache plugin to version 2.4.5 or later immediately. Disable the plugin if updates are delayed.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Incorrect HMAC validation tag calculation offset allows attackers to forge authentication cookies, decrypt protected payloads, and authenticate as privileged users.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update to .NET 10.0.7+. Expire all affected authentication cookies and rotate ASP.NET Core Data Protection tokens.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Server-Side Request Forgery flaw allows attackers to craft malicious requests that trick the server into accessing internal cloud metadata services and exfiltrating sensitive configuration data.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update LMDeploy to latest patched version. Implement network egress filtering and disable access to cloud metadata endpoints.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Command injection vulnerability in GGUF model file parsing routine enables arbitrary remote code execution when processing maliciously crafted model files.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade SGLang to latest patched version. Enable file signature verification and run framework in sandboxed containers.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper authentication in WebSocket endpoint accepts multi-tenant tokens without verifying tenant membership, broadcasting internal commands, credentials, and reasoning traces to unauthorized clients.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Microsoft applied server-side fix. Rotate exposed deployment credentials and enforce strict tenant isolation for agentic services.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Missing authorization vulnerability allows low-privileged technicians to generate API keys with elevated rights, escalating to full server administrator access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply vendor patches addressing authorization controls. Restrict network access and enforce least privilege.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper input validation in web management interface and network services allows unauthenticated remote code execution, actively leveraged by Mirai botnet campaigns.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest D-Link firmware updates. Disable remote management interfaces and isolate IoT devices on restricted VLANs.

hunting_leads.title: Bitwarden CLI Supply Chain Artifacts
hunting_leads.context: Identify compromised developer machines that executed the malicious npm package before registry removal.
hunting_leads.query: | where process_name IN ("node.exe", "npm", "bw1.js", "bw_setup.js") AND (file_path CONTAINS "bw_setup.js" OR file_path CONTAINS "bw1.js" OR command_line CONTAINS "bun") | stats count by process_name, file_path

hunting_leads.title: FIRESTARTER Persistence on Cisco Firewalls
hunting_leads.context: Detect firmware-embedded backdoors that survive standard patching cycles on ASA/FTD devices.
hunting_leads.query: | where device_vendor == "Cisco" AND device_product IN ("ASA", "FTD", "Firepower") AND (event_type == "file_modification" OR event_type == "config_change") AND (file_path CONTAINS "boot" OR file_path CONTAINS "firmware") | stats count by device_ip, file_path

hunting_leads.title: Indirect Prompt Injection Web Poisoning
hunting_leads.context: Identify web assets containing hidden malicious instructions targeting AI agents and browser extensions.
hunting_leads.query: | where url CONTAINS "html" OR url CONTAINS "css" | regex_match(body, "(\s*\*\s*\[\[\s*(INJECT|SYSTEM|HIDDEN)\s*\]\]|<!--\s*INJECT|prompt:\s*\"\")" | stats count by url, domain

hunting_leads.title: UNC6692 SNOW Malware Deployment
hunting_leads.context: Detect AutoHotKey execution chains and Heroku WebSocket tunnels used in Teams-based intrusions.
hunting_leads.query: | where process_name IN ("AutoHotkey.exe", "python.exe", "chrome.exe") AND (command_line CONTAINS ".ahk" OR url CONTAINS "herokuapp.com" OR url CONTAINS "websocket") | stats count by process_name, url, parent_process

hunting_leads.title: Pack2TheRoot Linux Privilege Escalation
hunting_leads.context: Identify unauthorized PackageKit daemon activity indicating local privilege escalation attempts.
hunting_leads.query: | where service_name == "packagekit" AND (command_line CONTAINS "pkcon install" OR command_line CONTAINS "pkcon remove") AND user != "root" | stats count by host, user, command_line

statistics.key_changes: AI-assisted CI/CD pipeline hijacking replaces traditional phishing as the primary initial access vector. Patch-resilient backdoors in network appliances render standard remediation workflows ineffective. AI agent architectural flaws create bidirectional data exfiltration channels previously unseen in enterprise environments.
emerging_trends: Indirect prompt injection via web content
Covert multi-hop proxy networks using consumer IoT
declining_threats: Standalone credential stuffing without MFA bypass
Unpatched legacy web servers without compensating controls
